title: Review Implement TUI Operator Action Handlers

handoff_summary.completed: Inspected `src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py`, `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py`, `tests/test_tui.py`, `tests/test_orchestrator.py`, `README.md`, and `specs/done/tui-operator-console-v1.md`. Verified the operator status-update path blocks manual `done` transitions for developer beads, confirmed regression coverage for the denial path in the TUI tests, and ran `python -m unittest tests.test_tui tests.test_orchestrator` successfully (`Ran 129 tests`, `OK`).
handoff_summary.remaining: No review findings remain in scope.
handoff_summary.risks: Residual risk is limited to untested behaviors outside the targeted TUI/operator-status and scheduler regression surface reviewed here.
handoff_summary.block_reason: Review reported unresolved findings in remaining.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None beyond normal shared-worktree coordination; this review made no repository changes.

conflict_risks: None beyond normal shared-worktree coordination; this review made no repository changes.

execution_history.summary: Review found a workflow-breaking defect: the TUI status-update path can mark a developer bead `done`, but that shortcut only flips the stored status and skips the scheduler completion path that creates tester/docs/review follow-up beads.

Reviewed the TUI operator action handler implementation and found no acceptance-blocking issues. The developer-bead `done` shortcut is now explicitly rejected, docs/specs are aligned with that restriction, and the targeted regression suite passes. Review reported unresolved findings and requires follow-up.

guardrails.template_text:
# Review Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Inspect code, tests, docs, and acceptance criteria for correctness, completeness, and risk.

Allowed actions:
- Review changed files and call out bugs, regressions, missing tests, and documentation gaps.
- Validate acceptance criteria against the implementation and handoff state.
- Block with a clear recommendation when the bead actually requires implementation work.

Disallowed actions:
- Implement feature work, tests, or docs instead of reporting findings.
- Rewrite architecture or silently fix issues discovered during review.
- Mark incomplete work as accepted without evidence.

Expected outputs:
- Review findings ordered by severity, or an explicit statement that no findings were discovered.
- Clear blocked handoff details when the task belongs to another agent type.

worker_prompt_context.title: Review Implement TUI Operator Action Handlers
worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks: The fix will likely touch the same operator-status logic in `src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py` and `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py`, plus scheduler/workflow tests in `tests/test_tui.py` and `tests/test_orchestrator.py`. If behavior changes, README/spec wording for the documented `done` shortcut must be kept in sync.

handoff_summary.completed: Reviewed the implementation in `src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py` and `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py`, inspected the associated docs in `README.md` and `specs/done/tui-operator-console-v1.md`, and checked regression coverage in `tests/test_tui.py` and `tests/test_orchestrator.py`. Ran `python -m unittest tests.test_tui tests.test_orchestrator` successfully (128 tests, passing).
handoff_summary.remaining: Restrict or redesign the `done` operator action so it cannot leave workflow state inconsistent. Today `apply_operator_status_update()` in [src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py](/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.orchestrator/agent-runs/B0044-review/repo/src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py#L100) only sets `bead.status` and writes an `updated` event, while normal completion in [src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py](/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.orchestrator/agent-runs/B0044-review/repo/src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py#L537) also records completion and creates follow-up tester/documentation/review beads in [src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py](/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.orchestrator/agent-runs/B0044-review/repo/src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py#L574). Add a regression test covering operator-driven `done` transitions for developer beads.
handoff_summary.risks: If shipped as-is, an operator can use the documented `u` + `d` + `y` path to mark implementation work complete without spawning required validation/documentation/review beads. That can silently bypass the orchestration pipeline and leave feature work merged without downstream gates.
handoff_summary.next_action: Hand off to a developer to fix the `done` operator action semantics and add regression coverage for the developer-bead completion workflow.
handoff_summary.block_reason: Operator-driven `done` status updates bypass scheduler completion side effects, so the review cannot accept this implementation.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: The fix will likely touch the same operator-status logic in `src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py` and `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py`, plus scheduler/workflow tests in `tests/test_tui.py` and `tests/test_orchestrator.py`. If behavior changes, README/spec wording for the documented `done` shortcut must be kept in sync.

last_agent_result.summary: Reviewed the TUI operator action handler implementation and found no acceptance-blocking issues. The developer-bead `done` shortcut is now explicitly rejected, docs/specs are aligned with that restriction, and the targeted regression suite passes. Review reported unresolved findings and requires follow-up.
